name: Phi Krasue
description: The Phi Krasue is a nocturnal female spirit of Southeast Asian folklore, particularly prominent in Thailand. It manifests as a detached woman's head, usually with her entrails hanging below. The Krasue is said to be cursed or transformed due to transgressions in a past life, or through the improper practice of dark magic. She floats through the air, emitting a faint, eerie glow, often seen in rural areas, especially near villages and rice paddies. The Krasue is feared for her insatiable hunger, particularly for blood and raw flesh. She is known to prey on pregnant women and newborns, consuming their placentas and blood. Villagers often take precautions to protect themselves from the Krasue, such as placing thorny branches around their homes or painting them with protective symbols. The origin of the Krasue varies in different local legends, but it consistently involves a woman who met a tragic end or misused supernatural powers. The Krasue represents the dangers of unchecked desires and the consequences of dark magic. It serves as a cautionary tale, highlighting the importance of morality and respect for the natural order. The spirit's terrifying appearance and malicious behavior have made it a staple of Thai horror stories and films.
subtitle: Floating head with entrails.
appearance: The Phi Krasue appears as a woman's head, often described as beautiful even in death, with long, dark hair. Her neck is severed, and dangling below are her internal organs – the lungs, stomach, and intestines – which glow with an ethereal light. The organs are often depicted as slimy and dripping with blood. The head and entrails float independently, without a body.
habitat: Rural areas, villages, rice paddies, and dark forests in Thailand and other Southeast Asian countries. She is most active at night and avoids populated areas during the day.
behavior: The Phi Krasue is a solitary creature, driven by hunger and a desire to consume blood and raw flesh. She is particularly drawn to pregnant women and newborns, whose blood and placentas sustain her. She is cunning and stealthy, able to slip through cracks and crevices to reach her victims. The Krasue is also known to spread disease and misfortune, bringing illness and death to those who cross her path. She is easily startled and will flee if confronted with strong light or protective symbols.

keyMyths.title: The Cursed Woman
keyMyths.description: A woman practicing black magic accidentally cursed herself, transforming into the Krasue after her death. Her head and entrails detached from her body, forever bound to roam the night in search of sustenance.

keyMyths.title: The Unfinished Meal
keyMyths.description: A pregnant woman died before finishing a meal containing dark magic ingredients. Her spirit became the Krasue, forever craving the blood and flesh she was unable to consume in life.

sources.author: S.J. Tambiah
sources.work: Buddhism and the Spirit Cults in North-East Thailand
sources.description: Anthropological study of spirit beliefs in Thailand.
